testCases: TC1: input { totalWatts: 4800, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { total: 43.64, breakdown: { totalWatts: 4800, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', baseCurrent: 43.64, continuousLoad: false, multiplier: 1, adjustedCurrent: 43.64 } }

TC2: input { totalWatts: 4800, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: true } → output { total: 54.55, breakdown: { totalWatts: 4800, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', baseCurrent: 43.64, continuousLoad: true, multiplier: 1.25, adjustedCurrent: 54.55 } }

TC3: input { totalWatts: 10000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { total: 26.24, breakdown: { totalWatts: 10000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three_phase', baseCurrent: 26.24, continuousLoad: false, multiplier: 1, adjustedCurrent: 26.24 } }

TC4: input { totalWatts: 10000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three_phase', continuousLoad: true } → output { total: 32.80, breakdown: { totalWatts: 10000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three_phase', baseCurrent: 26.24, continuousLoad: true, multiplier: 1.25, adjustedCurrent: 32.80 } }
TC5: input { totalWatts: 0, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { error: "Total watts must be greater than zero." }
TC6: input { totalWatts: -500, voltage: '220v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { error: "Total watts must be greater than zero." }
TC7: input { totalWatts: "abc", voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { error: "Total watts must be a number." }
TC8: input { totalWatts: 1000, voltage: '', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { error: "Invalid voltage. Must be 110V or 220V." }
TC9: input { totalWatts: 1000, voltage: '240v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { error: "Invalid voltage. Must be 110V or 220V." }
TC10: input { totalWatts: 1000, voltage: '110v', phase: '', continuousLoad: false } → output { error: "Invalid phase. Must be single_phase or three_phase." }
TC11: input { totalWatts: 1000, voltage: '110v', phase: 'two_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { error: "Invalid phase. Must be single_phase or three_phase." }

TC12: input { totalWatts: 2000, voltage: '110', phase: 'single', continuousLoad: false } → output { total: 18.18, breakdown: { totalWatts: 2000, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', baseCurrent: 18.18, continuousLoad: false, multiplier: 1, adjustedCurrent: 18.18 } }

TC13: input { totalWatts: 3000, voltage: '220 V', phase: 'Single Phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { total: 13.64, breakdown: { totalWatts: 3000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'single_phase', baseCurrent: 13.64, continuousLoad: false, multiplier: 1, adjustedCurrent: 13.64 } }

TC14: input { totalWatts: 4000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three', continuousLoad: true } → output { total: 13.12, breakdown: { totalWatts: 4000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three_phase', baseCurrent: 10.50, continuousLoad: true, multiplier: 1.25, adjustedCurrent: 13.12 } }

TC15: input { totalWatts: 99999, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { total: 909.08, breakdown: { totalWatts: 99999, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', baseCurrent: 909.08, continuousLoad: false, multiplier: 1, adjustedCurrent: 909.08 } }

TC16: input { totalWatts: 5000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three_phase', continuousLoad: true } → output { total: 16.40, breakdown: { totalWatts: 5000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three_phase', baseCurrent: 13.12, continuousLoad: true, multiplier: 1.25, adjustedCurrent: 16.40 } }

TC17: input { totalWatts: 1, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { total: 0.01, breakdown: { totalWatts: 1, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', baseCurrent: 0.01, continuousLoad: false, multiplier: 1, adjustedCurrent: 0.01 } }

TC18: input { totalWatts: 1000, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: 'false' } → continuousLoad string 'false' is truthy → output { total: 11.36, breakdown: ... } (since Boolean('false') = true) — acceptable; validation does not restrict to boolean only.
TC19: input { totalWatts: 5000, voltage: '220v', phase: 'three phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { total: 13.12, breakdown: ... } (spacing replaced)
TC20: input { totalWatts: NaN, voltage: '110v', phase: 'single_phase', continuousLoad: false } → output { error: "Total watts must be a number." }
